name: CIALIS Label Safety Boundary Extract
population_or_context: Labeled ED and BPH use in men; used as safety boundary context for off-label tadalafil questions in mostly healthy men age 30 to 50.
sample_size: Clinical trials section reports tadalafil exposure in over 9000 men worldwide; label also includes postmarketing reports of uncertain denominator.
methods: Structured extraction from current U.S. prescribing information revised March 2026.
population_profile: Label populations are primarily men treated for ED and/or BPH, with some clinical pharmacology healthy-subject data; direct for medication safety boundaries but indirect for off-label cognition, longevity, exercise, or mood enhancement in healthy men age 30 to 50.
tadalafil_exposure: CIALIS oral tadalafil 2.5 mg, 5 mg, 10 mg, or 20 mg; as-needed dosing up to once per day in most patients and once-daily dosing for ED/BPH; continuous exposure is highlighted for daily use.
indication_context: On-label ED and BPH prescribing information used as regulatory safety context for off-label interpretation.
study_design: Regulatory label summarizing clinical trials, clinical pharmacology studies, contraindications, warnings, precautions, adverse reactions, postmarketing experience, and special populations.
comparator_context: Placebo-controlled trial rates where provided; regulatory contraindication and warning statements otherwise have no comparator.
safety_findings: Contraindicated with organic nitrates and guanylate-cyclase stimulators such as riociguat; caution or dose limits with alpha-blockers, antihypertensives, substantial alcohol, potent CYP3A4 inhibitors, renal impairment, hepatic impairment, bleeding disorders/active peptic ulceration, NAION risk factors, hearing loss, and priapism risk factors. Common adverse reactions include headache, dyspepsia, back pain, myalgia, nasal congestion, flushing, and limb pain.
directness_to_target_population: High directness for current safety rules around tadalafil exposure; lower directness for estimating absolute risk in mostly healthy men age 30 to 50 using tadalafil off label because trial populations and indications differ.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Label trial rates may not reflect real-world use, combined off-label stacking, unmeasured rare events, or long-term enhancement use; postmarketing events have uncertain denominators and cannot establish causality.
